creator: philip@FreeBSD.org
text:
There is no completely reliable way to determine whether the purported sender of an email to a mailing list actually sent it.  It's almost impossible to prevent rejection messages to become a source of backscatter spam.

When a message makes it as far as the mailing list, we can no longer reject it without risking backscatter.  Discarding is the only option for messages that are not acceptable on the mailing list.

Your most recent message was discarded by mlmmj because it contained a large non-text attachment.  It was otherwise acceptable to all the filtering infrastructure.
